title: Vashan (language)
content:
{{Top|ca|leg|notoc=1}}
{{Language
|image=
|name=Vashan
|users=[[Vashan]]s<ref name="Endor">{{GamerCite|9|Endor and the Moddell Sector}}</ref>

'''Vashan''' was a [[Language/Legends|language]] used by the [[Vashan]]s, a [[Sentience/Legends|sentient]] [[Species/Legends|species]] [[Homeworld/Legends|native]] to the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Vasha/Legends|Vasha]]<ref name="Endor" /> of the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim Territories]].<ref name="EAOC">{{SW|url=news/star-wars-the-essential-atlas-online-companion|text=''Star Wars: The Essential Atlas'' Online Companion|int=Star Wars: The Essential Atlas Online Companion}} — Based on corresponding data for [[Vasha system/Legends|Vasha system]]</ref> It had a spoken form and a [[Writing/Legends|written]] form, and, in general, the Vashans knew both.<ref name="Endor" /> One specific member of the species who knew both spoken and written Vashan was the [[Sword/Legends|swordsman]] [[Poina Tuc-shol]]. [[Saqc'or]], a [[Svivreni]] [[Miner/Legends|miner]] and [[metallurgist]] on the planet [[Thonner/Legends|Thonner]], and [[Groex Gal]], an [[Ebranite]] [[Scout/Legends|scout]], [[Mountain/Legends|mountaineer]], and tour guide on the [[Moon/Legends|moon]] [[Qina/Legends|Qina]], both could also speak—but not write—Vashan.<ref name="Tessent">{{GamerCite|9|Race for the Tessent}}</ref>

==Behind the scenes==
Vashan was introduced in the [[Star Wars Gamer 9|ninth issue]] of the ''[[Star Wars Gamer]]'' magazine<ref name="Endor" /> on [[February 22]], [[2002]].<ref name="GamerDate">{{SWArchive|url=gaming/other/rpg/news20020222.html|text=Starfighters Soar in Gamer #9}}</ref> Within the magazine, the language was mentioned in the source article "[[Endor and the Moddell Sector]]" as well as the [[Roleplaying game|roleplaying]] adventure "[[Race for the Tessent]]," both being written for use with [[Wizards of the Coast]]'s ''[[Star Wars Roleplaying Game]]''.<ref name="Endor" /><ref name="Tessent" /> In one instance in the latter source, the name of the language is misspelled as "Vashon."<ref name="Tessent" />
